task.title: Allocating a partitioned data set or sequential file from an existing file

titlealts.searchtitle: Allocating a PDS or sequential file from an existing file

task.shortdesc: You can allocate a partitioned or sequential data set from an existing data set.

keywords.indexterm: allocatingpartitioned data set from an existing filePDS from an existing filesequential file from an existing file

taskbody.prereq: You must be successfully logged on to a remote system.

step.cmd: Navigate to the partitioned or sequential data set that has the same characteristics as the one you want to allocate.
Select the data set and open the Property view to be sure that you have what you want.
Select Allocate Like from the menu.

info.note: You can also press Ctrl+1 to allocate a new partitioned data set or Ctrl+2 to allocate a new sequential data set.

step.cmd: Specify the name of the data set to be allocated.

step.stepresult: On the next wizard page, the selected data set name is shown and Copy characteristics from an existing data set is selected.
The name of the new data set and its characteristics are displayed for verification purposes. You can change the characteristics if necessary.
The data set is allocated and added to the Retrieved Data Sets filter.

step.cmd: When the data set is allocated, you can work with it as you would with other data sets.
